item.title: Unlinked_Pages
item.description: Unlinked Notes Cross collar grip break to takedown Collar grip break downward Grip break on upper back Double under Closed guard figure 8 Double ankle grab pass Knee slice Toreando Leg drag Closed guard standing up S grip Buggy choke Crusifix Straight ankle lock Two grip choke on turtle Close guard ...

item.title: Empty_Pages
item.description: Empty Notes Figure-four grip Gogoplata Kimura Canto choke North south Rubber guard Double leg .

item.title: Recently_Updated
item.description: Recently Updated Notes index Lockdown Sweep Single leg Ankle pick Fireman throw Arm drag Double leg Collar & Sleeve to takedown README Triangle Escape .

item.title: Pressure
item.description: When in Knee on belly, Turtle, Mount your need to remember to apply constant pressure to prevent opponent from moving and giving them ground for free.

item.title: Granby roll
item.description: When in Turtle and opponent is on side and not applying constant pressure you can roll over shoulder that is on the opponents side and regain Open guard.

item.title: Turtle
item.description: Lessons learned from attacker side: Pressure Turtle defence Variant of turtle is Panda Always have the hook and rolling direction be opposite sides! Starting positions Rear Side Reverse (Head) Attack targets Isolate hand for a forward arc roll Unbalance to gain a Hook Attack patterns Armbar / Sweep ...

item.title: Base
item.description: General Imagine that you have a direct line outward from the center of your torso. That is zone where you can influence your opponent the most.

item.title: Flat on back
item.description: If opponent is trying to lift you from your back a general defence to make yourself heavier is the straighten the back against the floor.

item.title: Positions of strength
item.description: Inner control In many positions like Standing and Closed guard you can have a lot superior control over the opponent when you hold the grips that are on the inner side.

item.title: Combat base
item.description: Combat base can be split into three distinct variations: Combat base high Sitting posture which can be used to maintain distance with your opponent when not wanting to fight Standing.
